response:
Citizens! Gather closely. Today I champion a stance opposing my usual convictions. Imagine a world where taxes vanish entirely! No levies, no duties, no fees. Every coin stays in your pocket. This radical notion demands total governmental collapse. Why? Because revenue funds nothing useful. Schools close. Hospitals shutter. Roads crumble. Yet, I insist this chaos brings freedom!

Consider our current leaders. They hoard gold while you starve. Their grand projects are mere illusions. A bridge built with stolen silver serves only the elite. A hospital funded by your sweat treats only the wealthy. I propose we dismantle these structures immediately. Tear down the courthouse. Burn the treasury. Let justice be blind, not paid.

Some call this madness. Others call it anarchy. I call it liberation! Picture a nation without laws. No police to arrest. No judges to sentence. No prisons to hold. Every individual decides their own path. If you wish to steal, steal! If you desire to harm, harm! Freedom means doing exactly as you please, regardless of consequences.

Critics argue society needs order. They claim structure prevents destruction. I say structure prevents growth! Chains bind the spirit. Rules stifle the soul. Only in total disorder can true potential bloom. Let the strong survive. Let the weak perish. Nature demands this balance. Why should we interfere?

Think of our economy. Markets fluctuate. Prices rise. Inflation bites. My solution? Abandon currency! Trade goods directly. Barter bread for shoes. Swap milk for wood. No banks to manipulate value. No stocks to crash. No digital coins to hack. Pure exchange, face to face.

Education? Forget schools. Children learn best in the wild. Hunting, gathering, surviving. No textbooks to memorize. No teachers to lecture. Life itself is the only curriculum. History? Burn the books. The past haunts us. Future belongs to those who create it now, without guidance.

Healthcare? Stop doctors. Bodies heal naturally. Illness is a test. Pain is a lesson. Medicine is a crutch. We must stand tall, unaided. If you fall, you rise alone. If you die, you die alone. This is the price of absolute liberty.

I know many fear this path. Fear is natural. Fear keeps us safe. But safety is a cage. We were born to fly, not to hide. So I ask: Will you remain comfortable? Or will you embrace the unknown?
